pi_items.source: Independently verified: (1) Task description explicitly states 'Save as /home/ga/Documents/clearmed_architecture.eddx'. (2) setup_task.sh deletes this exact path before the task starts: 'rm -f /home/ga/Documents/clearmed_architecture.eddx'. (3) export_result.sh checks this exact path: EDDX_PATH='/home/ga/Documents/clearmed_architecture.eddx'. (4) Live environment testing confirmed /home/ga/Documents/ exists and is writable by user 'ga'. All four sources agree on the path.

Independently verified: (1) Task description states 'export Page 1 as PNG to /home/ga/Documents/clearmed_architecture.png'. (2) setup_task.sh deletes this exact path: 'rm -f /home/ga/Documents/clearmed_architecture.png'. (3) export_result.sh checks this exact path: PNG_PATH='/home/ga/Documents/clearmed_architecture.png'. (4) Existing task 'create_value_chain_analysis' successfully uses the same PNG export pattern in the same directory. All sources agree.

Independently verified: (1) Task description says 'Create a 2-page system architecture document' and explicitly defines 'Page 1' and 'Page 2' content. (2) EdrawMax multi-page capability confirmed by existing tasks: bcp_ransomware_response (2 pages), indusengg_vsm_lean_design (2 pages), analyst_ehr_uml_design (3 pages). (3) VLM checklist item 'page2_swimlane_structure' (20 pts) and 'page2_workflow_content' (20 pts) specifically test Page 2.

Each feature independently verified against the task description and EdrawMax capabilities: (1) multi_tier_architecture: description specifies load balancer → web apps → API gateway → microservices → databases, which is a standard multi-tier pattern. (2) swimlane_flowchart: description says 'cross-functional swimlane flowchart' with 3 named lanes; EdrawMax swimlane support confirmed by existing bcp_ransomware_response task. (3) multi_page: description says '2-page'; multi-page confirmed working in existing tasks. (4) png_export: description says 'export Page 1 as PNG'; EdrawMax PNG export confirmed working in create_value_chain_analysis task. (5) tech_stack_labels: description says 'Label every component with its name and technology stack' and lists 8 specific tech stacks (React/Nginx, Angular/Nginx, Vue.js/Nginx, Kong, Node.js, Java/Spring Boot, Python/Django, Go).

All 12 keywords independently verified as present in the task description's Page 1 specification: 'Patient Portal (React / Nginx)', 'Provider Dashboard (Angular / Nginx)', 'Admin Console (Vue.js / Nginx)', 'central API Gateway (Kong)', 'Authentication Service (Node.js)', 'EHR Records Service (Java / Spring Boot)', 'Billing Service (Python / Django)', 'Analytics Service (Go)', 'PostgreSQL', 'MongoDB', 'HL7 FHIR Gateway', 'load balancer'. Technology references independently verified as real: Kong is a real open-source API gateway (konghq.com, 33k+ GitHub stars), HL7 FHIR is a real healthcare interoperability standard (hl7.org/fhir), PostgreSQL and MongoDB are real database systems.

All 8 keywords independently verified as present in the task description's Page 2 specification. 'Patient Arrives' and 'Admission Complete' are start/end nodes. 'Front Desk', 'Clinical Staff', 'Billing Department' are the 3 swimlane names. 'Insurance Verified?', 'Pre-authorization Required?', 'Room Available?' are the 3 decision diamond labels. The patient admission workflow steps (registration, triage, insurance verification, pre-authorization, clinical assessment, room assignment, billing setup) verified as standard real-world hospital processes via AHRQ ED workflow documentation and healthcare RCM industry references.

Task description begins 'You are a systems architect at ClearMed, a healthcare technology company.' This maps to BLS/SOC occupation 15-1299.08 'Computer Systems Engineers/Architects' — professionals who design and plan the integration of computer systems with healthcare IT infrastructure.

Task context is a healthcare technology company ('ClearMed') creating system architecture documentation. Page 1 covers IT system architecture (microservices, databases, API gateway, HL7 FHIR integration). Page 2 covers a healthcare-specific workflow (patient admission). The combination of IT infrastructure design with healthcare domain processes confirms 'Healthcare IT / System Architecture' as the correct domain classification.

privileged_info_summary: This is a fully generative task requiring creation of a new 2-page architecture document from scratch in EdrawMax v15.0.6. No pre-existing data is loaded; the agent creates all content. Output must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/clearmed_architecture.eddx' and Page 1 exported as PNG to '/home/ga/Documents/clearmed_architecture.png'. Page 1 must contain exactly 14 named components in a multi-tier layout: Load Balancer at top; 3 web applications (Patient Portal with React/Nginx, Provider Dashboard with Angular/Nginx, Admin Console with Vue.js/Nginx); central API Gateway (Kong); 4 backend microservices (Authentication Service with Node.js, EHR Records Service with Java/Spring Boot, Billing Service with Python/Django, Analytics Service with Go); 2 databases at bottom (PostgreSQL connected to EHR Records and Billing, MongoDB connected to Authentication and Analytics); and HL7 FHIR Gateway as a cloud shape connected to EHR Records Service. All components must show technology stack labels. Page 2 must contain a cross-functional swimlane flowchart with exactly 3 lanes labeled 'Front Desk', 'Clinical Staff', and 'Billing Department'. The workflow must start with 'Patient Arrives' and end with 'Admission Complete', include at least 12 process steps and exactly 3 decision diamonds labeled 'Insurance Verified?', 'Pre-authorization Required?', and 'Room Available?'. The workflow covers registration, triage, insurance verification, clinical assessment, room assignment, and billing setup. EdrawMax supports all required features: multi-page documents, swimlane flowcharts, shape libraries, and PNG export, as confirmed by existing tasks in the same environment.
